zh.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton是Thomas Charlton和Mrs. Thomas Charleton的女兒，於從1370年到1375年出生在英國史洛普爾哈德利。她於1393年與William Knightley在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾阿普利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1399年在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾德斯費爾德，埋葬在英國哈普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton是Thomas Charlton和Mrs. Thomas Charleton的女儿，于从1370年到1375年出生在英国史洛普爾哈德利。她于1393年与William Knightley在英国英格兰林可爾史爾阿普利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1399年在英国英格兰韋斯特 越可爾德斯費爾德，埋葬在英国哈普爾。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Charlton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): habitational name from North Charlton or Charlton near Bellingham (both Northumberland) or from any of the many other places so named (there are over ninety examples in England), from Old English Ceorlatūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) of the peasants’. Old English ceorl denoted originally a free peasant of the lowest rank, later (but probably already before the Norman conquest) a tenant in pure villeinage, a serf or bondsman.

variants.name: Carlton
meanings: English (northern and eastern England): habitational name from any of various places called Carlton (Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Durham, Lincolnshire, Leicestershire, Nottinghamshire, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, East Yorkshire, Yorkshire, and North Yorkshire) or Carleton (Cumberland, Lancashire, Norfolk, Yorkshire). All come from Old Norse karl ‘common man, peasant’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’ (compare Charlton ). Places spelled Carl(e)ton (as opposed to Charlton) are in areas of Scandinavian settlement, mostly in northern England.

variants.name: Carlin
meanings: Irish (North Ulster; now also common in Scotland): from Ó Cearbhalláin ‘descendant of Cearbhallán’, a diminutive of the personal name Cearbhall (see Carroll ).
Italian (Veneto): from a pet form of the personal name Carlo, Italian form of Charles .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton was born from 1370 to 1375, in Hadley, Shropshire, England as the daughter of Thomas Charlton and Mrs. Thomas Charleton. She married William Knightley in 1393, in Apley, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1399, in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton est née du 1370 au 1375, à Hadley, Shropshire, Angleterre, fille de Thomas Charlton et Mrs. Thomas Charleton. Elle a épousé William Knightley en 1393, à Apley, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1399, à Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Hampshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1393 in Apley, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom William Knightley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1399 in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hampshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne De Charltonはから1370年まで1375年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアハドリーでThomas CharltonとMrs. Thomas Charletonの娘として生まれた。1393年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアアプリーでWilliam Knightleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1399年にイギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアハッドエルスフィールドで死去し，イギリスハンプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton(은)는 1370년 부터 1375년 까지 영국 슈럽실르 하들리에서 Thomas Charlton(와)과 Mrs. Thomas Charleton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1393년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 아플리에서 William Knightley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1399년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 허더스필드에서 사망했으며 영국 함프실르에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com William Knightley em 1393, em Apley, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Anne De Charlton родилась с 1370 по 1375 в местности хадли, шропшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Thomas Charlton и Mrs. Thomas Charleton. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Knightley в 1393 г. в местности апли, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1399 г. в местности худдирсфиелд, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности хампшир, Англия.
